passages.title: My Morning
passages.text: I wake up at seven. I drink water and eat bread. Then I go to work. I am a teacher. I like my job. My students are kind.

questions.q: What does the writer eat? (type the word)

passages.title: The Cat
passages.text: This is my cat. Her name is Luna. She is small and black. She sleeps all day. At night she is awake. She likes fish.

passages.title: A Trip to the Market
passages.text: On Saturday, we usually go to the market near our house. It is busy but cheerful. My mother buys fresh vegetables and warm bread. I always look for sweet fruit. Sometimes a musician plays the guitar, and people stop to listen. We carry heavy bags home and cook lunch together.

questions.q: When do they go to the market?

passages.title: A Weekend Trip
passages.text: Last weekend, my friends and I decided to visit a small town by the sea. Although the weather was cloudy when we left, it cleared up by noon. We walked along the harbor, tried fresh seafood, and talked about our plans for the future. What I enjoyed most was watching the boats return in the evening. If I had more time, I would have stayed another night. I believe short trips like this help us relax and see life from a different angle.

questions.q: How was the weather when they left?
What did the writer enjoy most?

passages.title: Life in the City
passages.text: Many people dream of living in a big city, believing that it offers endless opportunities. It is true that cities provide better jobs, cultural events, and services that smaller towns cannot match. However, this convenience comes at a price. The cost of living is high, the streets are crowded, and the constant noise can be exhausting. In my opinion, whether city life suits you depends on what you value most. Those who enjoy a fast pace and variety will thrive, while people who prefer peace and space may feel trapped. What matters is choosing the environment that lets you live the way you truly want.

questions.q: According to the writer, what is a drawback of city life?
What does the writer say city life depends on?
